name: CareView Communications Inc
business: CareView Communications provides turnkey patient video monitoring solutions for hospitals and healthcare facilities. Its products include CareView Patient Safety System, SitterView, TeleMedView, and CareView Connect for long-term care. It serves hospitals, nursing homes, adult living centers, and outpatient care facilities.

exposures.sentence: We offer a unique solution to clinical video monitoring, sitter reduction, and fall prevention by leveraging our patented Virtual Bed Rails and Virtual Chair Rails technology.

Connect Resident is a tablet application intended for the resident's direct use. This product currently supports video conferencing with a remote caregiver, becoming a communications conduit for telehealth.
CareView Connect leverages both passive and active sensors to track the activities of daily life.
the CareView Controller uses machine learning to differentiate between normal patient movements and behaviors of a patient at risk.

catalysts.event: Extended HealthTrust Purchasing Agreement through June 30, 2028
catalysts.sentence: On July 1, 2025, we extended our HealthTrust Purchasing Agreement through June 30, 2028.

catalysts.event: Fourteenth Amendment to Credit Agreement extending maturity to June 30, 2026
catalysts.sentence: On March 30, 2026 (the Effective Date), CareView Communications, Inc., the Borrower, PDL BioPharma, Inc.("PDL"), the Lender, Steven G. Johnson, President and Chief Executive Officer of the Company, and Dr. James R. Higgins, a director of the Company, entered into a Fourteenth Amendment to Credit Agreement (the Fourteenth Credit Agreement Amendment), pursuant to which the parties agreed to amend the Credit Agreement to (i) provide that the Maturity Date shall be extended to June 30, 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The skilled nursing home market consists of approximately 2,000,000 beds, which is double the size of the current hospital/healthcare facility bed market.
tamClaims.sentence: The skilled nursing home market consists of approximately 2,000,000 beds, which is double the size of the current hospital/healthcare facility bed market.

tamClaims.claim: The assisted living center market is even larger at approximately 3,000,000 beds.
tamClaims.sentence: The assisted living center market is even larger at approximately 3,000,000 beds.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 169 VA facilities with over 39,000 licensed beds and approximately 42 DOD hospitals with over 2,600 licensed beds.
tamClaims.sentence: the approximate 169 United States Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facilities with over 39,000 licensed beds and the approximate 42 DOD hospitals with over 2,600 licensed beds.
